name: Registry & Schema Store
description: Manages the .ledger/ directory structure (init), backend registration, and schema storage. `init` creates .ledger/registry/, .ledger/plans/, and .ledger/changelog.jsonl. Backend registration stores backend metadata (id, type, owner component) as flat YAML under .ledger/registry/, enforcing unique backend IDs and that no two components own the same backend. Schema store accepts schema YAML files and stores them verbatim (byte-for-byte) under .ledger/registry/<backend_id>/. Provides read APIs: list all schemas, get schemas by backend_id, get schema by backend_id+table. Schema validation checks all registered schemas for annotation conflict pairs, REQUIRES satisfaction (warnings vs errors), and backend ownership exclusivity — returns ALL violations. Appends entries to the append-only changelog (JSONL) for every backend registration and schema addition with timestamp, actor, change type, and affected backend/table/field.

types.name: BackendType
types.description: Supported backend types in the Ledger ecosystem.

types.name: ViolationSeverity
types.description: Severity level for schema validation violations.

types.name: ChangeType
types.description: Types of changes recorded in the append-only changelog.

types.name: Path
types.description: Filesystem path (pathlib.Path). Represents an absolute or relative directory/file path.

types.name: datetime
types.description: UTC datetime (datetime.datetime with tzinfo=UTC). All timestamps are ISO 8601 UTC.

types.name: BackendMetadata

fields.name: backend_id
fields.description: Unique identifier for the backend. Must match pattern ^[a-z][a-z0-9_-]{1,62}[a-z0-9]$ (3-64 chars, lowercase alphanumeric with hyphens/underscores, must start with letter and end with alphanumeric).

validators.error_message: backend_id must be 3-64 lowercase alphanumeric characters, hyphens, or underscores; must start with a letter and end with alphanumeric
backend_id must be between 3 and 64 characters

fields.name: backend_type
fields.description: The type of the backend system (e.g. postgres, redis, s3).

fields.name: owner_component
fields.description: The component that owns this backend. No two components may own the same backend.

validators.error_message: owner_component must be between 1 and 128 characters

fields.name: registered_at
fields.description: UTC timestamp of when this backend was registered. ISO 8601 format.

types.description: Frozen Pydantic v2 model representing registered backend metadata. Stored as flat YAML under .ledger/registry/<backend_id>.yaml.

types.name: SchemaRecord

fields.name: backend_id
fields.description: The backend this schema belongs to.

fields.name: table_name
fields.description: The table/entity name within the backend.

validators.error_message: table_name must be between 1 and 256 characters

fields.name: raw_content
fields.description: Verbatim byte-for-byte content of the schema YAML file as ingested. Never normalized, reformatted, or reordered.

fields.name: parsed_content
fields.description: YAML-parsed dictionary representation of raw_content, cached for query and validation use.

fields.name: stored_at
fields.description: UTC timestamp of when this schema was stored.

types.description: Pydantic v2 model holding a stored schema. raw_content preserves verbatim bytes; parsed_content is the YAML-parsed dict for query use.

types.name: Violation

fields.name: severity
fields.description: Whether this violation is a blocking error or an advisory warning.

fields.name: rule
fields.description: Machine-readable rule identifier (e.g. 'annotation_conflict', 'requires_unsatisfied', 'ownership_exclusive').

fields.name: backend_id
fields.description: The backend where the violation was detected.

fields.name: table
fields.description: Table name if the violation is table-scoped. Empty string if backend-level.

fields.name: field
fields.description: Field name if the violation is field-scoped. Empty string if table-level or backend-level.

fields.name: message
fields.description: Human-readable description of the violation.

types.description: Frozen Pydantic v2 model representing a single schema validation violation. Validation always collects ALL violations rather than short-circuiting on the first.

types.name: ValidationResult

fields.name: violations
fields.description: Complete list of all detected violations across all registered schemas.

fields.name: valid
fields.description: Computed property: True if no violations have severity='error'. Warnings alone do not invalidate.

types.description: Pydantic v2 model containing the full set of violations from validate_all(). The 'valid' property is True only when there are zero error-severity violations (warnings are acceptable).

types.name: ChangelogEntry

fields.name: timestamp
fields.description: UTC timestamp (ISO 8601) of when the change occurred.

fields.name: sequence
fields.description: Monotonically increasing sequence number for changelog ordering. Starts at 1.

validators.error_message: sequence must be a positive integer starting at 1

fields.name: actor
fields.description: Identity of the user or system that performed the change (e.g. CLI user, CI pipeline).

validators.error_message: actor must be between 1 and 256 characters

fields.name: change_type
fields.description: The type of change: 'backend_registered' or 'schema_added'.

fields.name: backend_id
fields.description: The backend affected by this change.

fields.name: table
fields.description: Table name if the change is schema-scoped. Empty string for backend-level changes.

fields.name: field
fields.description: Field name if the change is field-scoped. Empty string otherwise.

fields.name: detail
fields.description: Optional human-readable detail about the change.

types.description: Frozen Pydantic v2 model for a single line in the append-only JSONL changelog (.ledger/changelog.jsonl). Each entry records a mutation to the registry.

types.name: LedgerError

fields.name: message
fields.description: Human-readable error message.

types.description: Base exception for all Ledger registry errors. All custom exceptions inherit from this.

types.name: LedgerNotInitializedError

fields.name: message
fields.description: Error message including the expected path.

fields.name: root
fields.description: The root path where .ledger/ was expected.

types.description: Raised when an operation requires an initialized .ledger/ directory but none exists. Inherits from LedgerError.

types.name: LedgerCorruptedError

fields.name: message
fields.description: Error message describing the corruption.

fields.name: missing_paths
fields.description: List of expected paths that are missing or corrupted.

types.description: Raised when .ledger/ exists but is in a partial or corrupted state (e.g. missing required subdirectories). Inherits from LedgerError.

types.name: DuplicateBackendError

fields.name: backend_id
fields.description: The duplicate backend_id.

types.description: Raised when attempting to register a backend with an ID that already exists. Inherits from LedgerError.

types.name: OwnershipConflictError

fields.name: backend_id
fields.description: The contested backend_id.

fields.name: existing_owner
fields.description: The component that currently owns this backend.

fields.name: attempted_owner
fields.description: The component that attempted to claim ownership.

types.description: Raised when a backend registration would violate ownership exclusivity (no two components may own the same backend). Inherits from LedgerError.

types.name: BackendNotFoundError

fields.name: backend_id
fields.description: The backend_id that was not found.

types.description: Raised when referencing a backend_id that has not been registered. Inherits from LedgerError.

types.name: SchemaParseError

fields.name: backend_id
fields.description: The backend the schema was being stored for.

fields.name: table
fields.description: The table name of the schema.

fields.name: parse_error
fields.description: The underlying YAML parse error message.

types.description: Raised when schema YAML bytes cannot be parsed. Includes the file path and parse error details. Inherits from LedgerError.

functions.name: init
functions.description: Initializes the .ledger/ directory structure at the given root path. Creates .ledger/registry/, .ledger/plans/, and .ledger/changelog.jsonl. Idempotent: if all required paths already exist, returns without error. Raises LedgerCorruptedError if .ledger/ exists but is in a partial or inconsistent state (e.g. some subdirectories missing or changelog is not a file).

inputs.name: root
inputs.description: Root directory under which .ledger/ will be created.

error_cases.name: corrupted_state
error_cases.condition: .ledger/ directory exists but is missing required subdirectories or changelog file

error_data.missing_paths: List of paths that should exist but do not

preconditions: root must be an existing directory with write permissions
If .ledger/ exists at root, it must either be fully intact (all subdirs + changelog present) or not exist at all — partial state triggers LedgerCorruptedError
postconditions: .ledger/ directory exists at root
.ledger/registry/ directory exists
.ledger/plans/ directory exists
.ledger/changelog.jsonl file exists (created empty if new)
side_effects: Creates empty file .ledger/changelog.jsonl if it does not exist

structured_side_effects.description: Creates the registry directory for backend metadata and schema storage
Creates the plans directory for migration plans
Creates the empty append-only JSONL changelog file if it does not already exist

functions.name: register_backend
functions.description: Registers a new backend by writing its metadata as flat YAML to .ledger/registry/<backend_id>.yaml. Enforces unique backend_id (no duplicate registrations) and ownership exclusivity (no two components may own the same backend). On success, appends a 'backend_registered' entry to the changelog and returns it.

inputs.name: actor
inputs.description: Identity of the user or system performing the registration.

validators.error_message: actor must be between 1 and 256 characters

error_cases.name: not_initialized
error_cases.condition: .ledger/ directory does not exist at root

error_data.root: The root path where .ledger/ was expected

error_cases.name: duplicate_backend
error_cases.condition: A backend with the same backend_id is already registered

error_cases.name: ownership_conflict
error_cases.condition: The backend_id is already owned by a different component

preconditions: .ledger/ must be initialized at root (init has been called)
metadata.backend_id must not already be registered
No other backend may already be owned by a different component with the same backend_id
postconditions: File .ledger/registry/<backend_id>.yaml exists with the metadata serialized as YAML
A 'backend_registered' ChangelogEntry has been appended to .ledger/changelog.jsonl
Returned ChangelogEntry has change_type='backend_registered', matching backend_id, and monotonically increasing sequence number

structured_side_effects.description: Writes backend metadata as flat YAML
Appends a 'backend_registered' changelog entry with timestamp, actor, sequence, and backend_id
Scans existing backend YAML files to enforce uniqueness and ownership exclusivity (scan-on-write)

functions.name: store_schema
functions.description: Stores a schema YAML file verbatim (byte-for-byte) under .ledger/registry/<backend_id>/<table>.yaml. Validates that the raw bytes are parseable as YAML before storing. The backend must already be registered. On success, appends a 'schema_added' entry to the changelog and returns it.

inputs.name: backend_id
inputs.description: The registered backend to store the schema under.

validators.error_message: backend_id must match the required pattern
table name must be between 1 and 256 characters

inputs.name: raw_yaml
inputs.description: Raw YAML content to store verbatim. Must be parseable as valid YAML.

inputs.name: actor
inputs.description: Identity of the user or system storing the schema.

validators.error_message: actor must be between 1 and 256 characters

error_cases.name: not_initialized
error_cases.condition: .ledger/ directory does not exist at root

error_data.root: The root path where .ledger/ was expected

error_cases.name: backend_not_found
error_cases.condition: No backend with the given backend_id has been registered

error_data.backend_id: The backend_id that was not found

error_cases.name: schema_parse_error
error_cases.condition: raw_yaml bytes cannot be parsed as valid YAML

error_data.parse_error: The underlying YAML parse error message

preconditions: backend_id must reference an already-registered backend
raw_yaml must be valid YAML (parseable by pyyaml)
postconditions: File .ledger/registry/<backend_id>/<table>.yaml exists with exactly the bytes from raw_yaml
A 'schema_added' ChangelogEntry has been appended to .ledger/changelog.jsonl
Returned ChangelogEntry has change_type='schema_added', matching backend_id and table

structured_side_effects.description: Writes verbatim schema YAML bytes; never normalizes, reformats, or reorders
Creates the backend subdirectory if it does not already exist
Appends a 'schema_added' changelog entry with timestamp, actor, sequence, backend_id, and table
Reads backend metadata to verify the backend is registered before storing schema

functions.name: list_backends
functions.description: Returns a list of all registered backends, sorted by backend_id alphabetically. Reads all .yaml files directly under .ledger/registry/ and deserializes them as BackendMetadata.

error_cases.name: not_initialized
error_cases.condition: .ledger/ directory does not exist at root

error_data.root: The root path where .ledger/ was expected

postconditions: Returned list is sorted by backend_id in ascending lexicographic order
Each BackendMetadata in the list corresponds to a .yaml file in .ledger/registry/
No side effects on the filesystem

structured_side_effects.description: Reads all backend metadata YAML files in the registry directory

functions.name: list_schemas
functions.description: Returns a list of all schema records for the given backend_id, sorted by table_name alphabetically. Reads all .yaml files under .ledger/registry/<backend_id>/.

inputs.name: backend_id
inputs.description: The backend to list schemas for.

validators.error_message: backend_id must match the required pattern

error_cases.name: not_initialized
error_cases.condition: .ledger/ directory does not exist at root

error_data.root: The root path where .ledger/ was expected

error_cases.name: backend_not_found
error_cases.condition: No backend with the given backend_id has been registered

error_data.backend_id: The backend_id that was not found

preconditions: backend_id must reference a registered backend
postconditions: Returned list is sorted by table_name in ascending lexicographic order
Each SchemaRecord contains both raw_content (verbatim bytes) and parsed_content (dict)
No side effects on the filesystem

structured_side_effects.description: Reads all schema YAML files for the specified backend

functions.name: get_schema
functions.description: Returns a single SchemaRecord for the given backend_id and table name, or None if no schema exists for that combination. Does not raise an error if the schema is not found — returns None instead.

validators.error_message: backend_id must match the required pattern

inputs.name: table
inputs.description: The table/entity name to look up.

validators.error_message: table name must be between 1 and 256 characters

error_cases.name: not_initialized
error_cases.condition: .ledger/ directory does not exist at root

error_data.root: The root path where .ledger/ was expected

postconditions: If the schema file .ledger/registry/<backend_id>/<table>.yaml exists, returns a SchemaRecord with verbatim raw_content
If the schema file does not exist, returns None
No side effects on the filesystem

structured_side_effects.description: Reads a single schema file if it exists

functions.name: validate_all
functions.description: Scans all registered schemas across all backends and returns ALL validation violations. Checks: (1) annotation conflict pairs — certain annotations are mutually exclusive, (2) REQUIRES satisfaction — annotations that require other annotations or conditions produce warnings or errors, (3) backend ownership exclusivity — no two components may own the same backend. Returns a ValidationResult with the complete list of violations and a computed 'valid' flag that is True only if there are zero error-severity violations.

error_cases.name: not_initialized
error_cases.condition: .ledger/ directory does not exist at root

error_data.root: The root path where .ledger/ was expected

postconditions: Returned ValidationResult.violations contains every detected violation — validation never short-circuits
ValidationResult.valid is True if and only if no violation has severity='error'
No side effects on the filesystem — validation is read-only

structured_side_effects.description: Reads all backend metadata YAML files and all schema YAML files for cross-schema validation

functions.name: read_changelog
functions.description: Reads entries from the append-only JSONL changelog at .ledger/changelog.jsonl. Supports optional filtering by backend_id and an optional limit on the number of entries returned (most recent first when limited). Performs lazy line-by-line reading to avoid loading the entire file into memory.

inputs.name: backend_id
inputs.description: If non-empty, filter changelog entries to only those affecting this backend_id. Empty string means no filter.

inputs.name: limit
inputs.description: Maximum number of entries to return. 0 means no limit (return all matching entries).

error_cases.name: not_initialized
error_cases.condition: .ledger/ directory does not exist at root

error_data.root: The root path where .ledger/ was expected

preconditions: .ledger/changelog.jsonl must exist and be a valid JSONL file
postconditions: Returned list contains at most 'limit' entries if limit > 0
If backend_id filter is provided, all returned entries have matching backend_id
Entries are ordered by sequence number ascending
No side effects on the filesystem

structured_side_effects.description: Lazy line-by-line read of the append-only JSONL changelog

invariants: Schema YAML is stored verbatim — never normalized, reformatted, or reordered on ingestion
Backend IDs are unique across the entire registry; enforced both by scan-on-write and filesystem path uniqueness
No two components may own the same backend (ownership exclusivity)
The changelog at .ledger/changelog.jsonl is append-only; entries are never modified or deleted
Changelog sequence numbers are monotonically increasing starting at 1 with no gaps
validate_all returns ALL violations across all schemas — it never short-circuits on the first error
ValidationResult.valid is True if and only if there are zero error-severity violations; warnings do not invalidate
init is idempotent: calling it on an already-initialized .ledger/ with all paths intact is a no-op
All public functions require .ledger/ to be initialized and raise LedgerNotInitializedError otherwise
backend_id must match ^[a-z][a-z0-9_-]{1,62}[a-z0-9]$ (3-64 chars, lowercase, starts with letter, ends with alphanumeric)

data_access.rationale: Reads backend metadata YAML files (INTERNAL tier — contains owner_component mappings and backend type information) and schema YAML files (PUBLIC tier — user-provided schema definitions with annotation metadata). Writes backend metadata YAML, verbatim schema bytes, and append-only JSONL changelog entries to the .ledger/ directory structure, all classified as INTERNAL since they are local project configuration artifacts that may reference internal component ownership and obligation annotations.

side_effects.rationale: Writes backend metadata as flat YAML to .ledger/registry/<backend_id>.yaml during register_backend
Writes verbatim schema YAML bytes to .ledger/registry/<backend_id>/<table>.yaml during store_schema
Appends JSONL changelog entries to .ledger/changelog.jsonl on every backend registration and schema addition
Reads backend metadata YAML files for uniqueness checks, listing, and validation
Reads verbatim schema YAML files for listing, retrieval, and cross-schema validation

authority.rationale: Registry & Schema Store is the single authoritative owner of backend registration metadata, verbatim schema storage, and the append-only changelog. No other component may write to .ledger/registry/ or .ledger/changelog.jsonl. All other components must read backend and schema data through this component's read APIs.
